contents:
# Over 10,000 security personnel deployed for 48th ASEAN Summit in Cebu

## Overview
Philippine authorities deployed more than 10,000 security and emergency personnel across Central Visayas to secure the 48th ASEAN Summit. The Philippine National Police and Armed Forces of the Philippines lead the multi-agency effort covering land, air, maritime, and cyber domains. Quick response teams and armored units remain on standby for the event scheduled May 7-8 in Lapu-Lapu City.

## Details
Police Regional Office-7 stationed over 4,000 officers, supported by 3,000 from other uniformed services. AFP contributes over 1,000 personnel including overt/covert teams and medical responders. Col. Joel Benedict Batara, Joint Task Group-Cebu commander, stated multi-domain operations include Army, Air Force, Navy, and cybersecurity teams. Police Col. Kim Molitas serves as ASEAN spokesperson for PRO-7. Lt. Col. Israel Galorio noted AFP support for police and presidential security. A centralized command center coordinates all agencies as of April 30.

**Location**: Cebu City, Philippines

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @BeltelFreeAudio, which covers Philippines security regional news ASEAN diplomacy. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Philippines security regional news ASEAN diplomacy will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
